page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-192GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 32 shared vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This server does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU resources, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average multi-core capabilities alongside weak single-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU test ranks in the bottom 10%, whereas the multi-core CPU test falls within the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks demonstrate average throughput, with read-only large block speeds reaching 125.11 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations show notable strength, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression remains in the bottom 25%. Cryptographic performance is also strong, as evidenced by a top 25% score in the PassMark CPU encryption test.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model and the absence of bundled local storage position this server as a resource-dense option for memory-heavy workloads that do not require constant, dedicated single-core CPU performance. It is suitable for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and moderate web serving, but less optimal for single-threaded tasks or workloads requiring high-performance local NVMe storage.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-192GB is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor, equipped with 32 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 192.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides 6.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between weak single-core execution, which ranks in the bottom 10% for stress-ng, and average to strong multi-threaded capabilities, such as a top 25% ranking in bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and PassMark CPU encryption. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently perform in the middle 50% tier. Given its shared CPU allocation and high memory capacity, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads, including database operations and static web serving, where sustained single-core peak performance is not a primary requirement.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-192GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering balanced multi-threaded performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-192GB is a KVM virtual server with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-memory virtual server featuring thirty-two shared cores and average multi-threaded performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory (6.0 GB per core)
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Strong multi-threaded decompression and encryption performance
Best suited for database operations and memory-heavy workloads
